name: TAO Trend Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: deeper pullback reset inside an uptrend followed by value reclaim and continuation
thesis: TAO 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether a stronger-trending asset can resume after a deeper reset, provided price reclaims fast value and directional pressure rotates back with it. The edge is not buying shallow EMA taps; it is buying repaired pullbacks after inventory is reset.
invalidation_condition: Abort if deeper resets rarely reclaim fast value, if reclaimed pullbacks fail to extend after costs, or if signals become too sparse.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Uptrend pauses with a deeper pullback into value, then resumes once price reclaims the fast balance reference and directional pressure re-aligns.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A low-confidence trend-up backdrop often produces messy but tradable repaired pullbacks. TAO should express that mechanism more clearly than ETH because its swings are larger.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because the pullback-reset structure is large enough to matter and still slow enough to avoid intrabar noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If trend quality is weaker than advertised, deeper pullbacks may become outright reversals instead of repaired resets.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are valid but second-leg extension is too brief, reduce target ambition and shorten the runner rather than tightening pullback depth.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable number of repaired-pullback entries with front-half exits doing most of the expectancy and occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h and at least one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after value reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or reclaimed pullbacks repeatedly fail to print a second leg.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reset depth, reclaim, and directional handoff are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using broad reset logic rather than exact candle choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for a repaired deeper-pullback continuation mechanism in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Credible density and positive continuation after reclaim.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Pullbacks reclaim but fail to extend, or the setup is too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sharp resets followed by a visible second-leg continuation after fast value reclaim.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism is structural on a cleaner large-cap lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows similar repaired-pullback continuation with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only survives on TAO volatility and disappears on ETH.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner repaired-pullback continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same continuation grammar survives on a more fragile high-beta lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS preserves the repaired continuation shape without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS becomes structurally noisy or too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Less frequent but punchier repaired-continuation moves than ETH.
